entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note:
First **≥128-bit-secure** baseline. A TFHE programmable bootstrap (CGGI blind rotation +
sample-extract + key-switch) over an approximate f64 complex-FFT negacyclic multiplier
(folded length-N/2 FFT). Parameters chosen to clear the harness's classical core-SVP gate:

- input/output LWE: `n=1024`, `σ=2^44` → **130.8-bit** core-SVP.
- GLWE: `k=1, N=2048` (dim 2048), `σ=2^29` → **163.8-bit** core-SVP.
- `pbs ℓ=2 baselog=12`, `ks ℓ=5 baselog=4`, 4-message space.

The large LWE noise needed for security is rounded away by the mod-switch on input; the
key-switch / bootstrap-key noise lands in the output, leaving a comfortable margin
(~7-9 bits) below the message gap.
entries.approach:
First **≥128-bit-secure** baseline. A TFHE programmable bootstrap (CGGI blind rotation +
sample-extract + key-switch) over an approximate f64 complex-FFT negacyclic multiplier
(folded length-N/2 FFT). Parameters chosen to clear the harness's classical core-SVP gate:

- input/output LWE: `n=1024`, `σ=2^44` → **130.8-bit** core-SVP.
- GLWE: `k=1, N=2048` (dim 2048), `σ=2^29` → **163.8-bit** core-SVP.
- `pbs ℓ=2 baselog=12`, `ks ℓ=5 baselog=4`, 4-message space.

The large LWE noise needed for security is rounded away by the mod-switch on input; the
key-switch / bootstrap-key noise lands in the output, leaving a comfortable margin
(~7-9 bits) below the message gap.
entries.evalSnapshot:
params: n=1024 k=1 N=2048 | LWE σ=2^44.0 dim 1024 → 130.8 bits | GLWE σ=2^29.0 dim 2048 → 163.8 bits
security gate: OK (≥128 core-SVP bits)
fixture           got want    margin  ok
identity/m0         0    0     9.4b  OK
increment/m0        1    1     9.4b  OK
identity/m1         1    1     6.8b  OK
increment/m1        2    2     6.6b  OK
identity/m2         2    2     7.6b  OK
increment/m2        3    3     7.7b  OK
identity/m3         3    3     8.9b  OK
increment/m3        0    0     9.4b  OK
--------------------------------------------

SCORE: 49499417 ns/bootstrap  (median of 21; best 48340084 ns) — LOWER IS BETTER
       = 49.499 ms

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note:
rs-tfhe-style **N=1024** parameters (the [rs-tfhe](https://github.com/thedonutfactory/rs-tfhe)
128-bit gate-bootstrap shape), adapted to this challenge's q=2^64 / 4-message space. Halving
the ring degree from the N=2048 baseline roughly halves the FFT work. Same f64-FFT CGGI
bootstrap; **only `src/algorithm/` changed** (parameters + decomposition).

- LWE: `n=768`, `σ=2^46` → **132-bit**. (rs-tfhe uses n=700; our 4-message space has a
  tighter noise budget than its boolean gate, so a slightly larger n lets `lwe_sigma` drop
  and cuts key-switch noise.)
- GLWE: `k=1, N=1024`, `σ=2^39` → **129.6-bit**.
- `pbs ℓ=3 baselog=7` (21-bit), `ks ℓ=8 baselog=2` (fine key-switch). Output noise σ≈2^55.3
  ⇒ failure margin ~4.7 bits (≈ 2^-130).

The SPQLIOS AVX/FMA assembly FFT that gives rs-tfhe its ~15 ms is a C++/assembly build
(`cc` + `build.rs`) outside `src/algorithm/`, so it cannot be ported here; with the
generic rustfft this lands ~32 ms. A hand-written `core::arch` AVX FFT inside `src/algorithm/`
(allowed) is the path toward rs-tfhe's speed.
entries.approach:
rs-tfhe-style **N=1024** parameters (the [rs-tfhe](https://github.com/thedonutfactory/rs-tfhe)
128-bit gate-bootstrap shape), adapted to this challenge's q=2^64 / 4-message space. Halving
the ring degree from the N=2048 baseline roughly halves the FFT work. Same f64-FFT CGGI
bootstrap; **only `src/algorithm/` changed** (parameters + decomposition).

- LWE: `n=768`, `σ=2^46` → **132-bit**. (rs-tfhe uses n=700; our 4-message space has a
  tighter noise budget than its boolean gate, so a slightly larger n lets `lwe_sigma` drop
  and cuts key-switch noise.)
- GLWE: `k=1, N=1024`, `σ=2^39` → **129.6-bit**.
- `pbs ℓ=3 baselog=7` (21-bit), `ks ℓ=8 baselog=2` (fine key-switch). Output noise σ≈2^55.3
  ⇒ failure margin ~4.7 bits (≈ 2^-130).

The SPQLIOS AVX/FMA assembly FFT that gives rs-tfhe its ~15 ms is a C++/assembly build
(`cc` + `build.rs`) outside `src/algorithm/`, so it cannot be ported here; with the
generic rustfft this lands ~32 ms. A hand-written `core::arch` AVX FFT inside `src/algorithm/`
(allowed) is the path toward rs-tfhe's speed.
entries.algoChanges: src/algorithm/mod.rs: params() — N 2048->1024, n 1024->768, retuned σ/decomposition
entries.evalSnapshot:
params: n=768 k=1 N=1024 | LWE σ=2^46.0 dim 768 → 132.0 bits | GLWE σ=2^39.0 dim 1024 → 129.6 bits
security gate: OK (≥128 bits, standard model)
fixture           got want  ok
identity/m0         0    0  OK
increment/m0        1    1  OK
identity/m1         1    1  OK
increment/m1        2    2  OK
identity/m2         2    2  OK
increment/m2        3    3  OK
identity/m3         3    3  OK
increment/m3        0    0  OK
--------------------------------------------
output noise: σ=2^55.3, failure margin 4.7 bits (need ≥3.5)

SCORE: 32413625 ns/bootstrap  (median of 21; best 31723917 ns) — LOWER IS BETTER
       = 32.414 ms

entries.author: @10d9e
entries.note:
Rewrote the bootstrap inner loop (`src/algorithm/fft.rs` + `mod.rs`); **params unchanged**
from entry 0001 (still 128-bit, σ-margin ~4.6 bits). Benchmarking [rs-tfhe](https://github.com/thedonutfactory/rs-tfhe)
on the same machine was the key: it hits **14.8 ms / gate with rustfft** (its SPQLIOS AVX path
is commented out), and its per-FFT cost (~1.9 µs) is close to ours — so the gap was **not the
FFT** but the per-CMux glue. Fixes, in order of impact:

1. **Inline, batched gadget decomposition** — was 2048 `decompose()` calls (each returning an
   `[i64;8]`) per CMux; now one inline pass with sequential writes. (Biggest win.)
2. **Monomial rotation via `copy_from_slice`** — two contiguous regions (vectorized memcpy +
   a negation loop) instead of branchy scattered writes.
3. **Split-format (SoA) spectra** end-to-end (`[re(M)|im(M)]`) so the GGSW external-product
   pointwise MAC auto-vectorizes (matches rs-tfhe's `fma_in_fd`).
4. **Hand-written radix-4 SIMD FFT** (NEON / AVX2+FMA / scalar, runtime-dispatched; fused
   negacyclic twist/untangle; bit-reversal folded into the twist-pack load). Naive-DFT unit
   test pins correctness.

Result on this arm64 (M3): **32.4 → ~22 ms** (~32% faster). Remaining gap to rs-tfhe's 14.8 ms
is structural: our n=768 (vs 700, +10%) and 4-message space, the q=2^64 float→torus reduce
(vs their q=2^32), and `Vec<Vec<u64>>` vs fixed `[T;N]` poly layout. KS+extract is ~1.85 ms
(comparable to theirs). x86 uses the 4-wide AVX2 FFT path (validated via cross-compile; measured on CI).
entries.approach:
Rewrote the bootstrap inner loop (`src/algorithm/fft.rs` + `mod.rs`); **params unchanged**
from entry 0001 (still 128-bit, σ-margin ~4.6 bits). Benchmarking [rs-tfhe](https://github.com/thedonutfactory/rs-tfhe)
on the same machine was the key: it hits **14.8 ms / gate with rustfft** (its SPQLIOS AVX path
is commented out), and its per-FFT cost (~1.9 µs) is close to ours — so the gap was **not the
FFT** but the per-CMux glue. Fixes, in order of impact:

1. **Inline, batched gadget decomposition** — was 2048 `decompose()` calls (each returning an
   `[i64;8]`) per CMux; now one inline pass with sequential writes. (Biggest win.)
2. **Monomial rotation via `copy_from_slice`** — two contiguous regions (vectorized memcpy +
   a negation loop) instead of branchy scattered writes.
3. **Split-format (SoA) spectra** end-to-end (`[re(M)|im(M)]`) so the GGSW external-product
   pointwise MAC auto-vectorizes (matches rs-tfhe's `fma_in_fd`).
4. **Hand-written radix-4 SIMD FFT** (NEON / AVX2+FMA / scalar, runtime-dispatched; fused
   negacyclic twist/untangle; bit-reversal folded into the twist-pack load). Naive-DFT unit
   test pins correctness.

Result on this arm64 (M3): **32.4 → ~22 ms** (~32% faster). Remaining gap to rs-tfhe's 14.8 ms
is structural: our n=768 (vs 700, +10%) and 4-message space, the q=2^64 float→torus reduce
(vs their q=2^32), and `Vec<Vec<u64>>` vs fixed `[T;N]` poly layout. KS+extract is ~1.85 ms
(comparable to theirs). x86 uses the 4-wide AVX2 FFT path (validated via cross-compile; measured on CI).
entries.algoChanges:
src/algorithm/fft.rs:  rustfft → hand radix-4 SIMD FFT; split-format spectra; fused twist/untangle
src/algorithm/mod.rs:  inline batched decompose; copy_from_slice rotation; split-format pointwise MAC
